topics.titleEn: Kubernetes Autoscaling: HPA, VPA, and CA
topics.angle: guide + combining HPA, VPA, and Cluster Autoscaler without them fighting each other
topics.brief: Sections: HPA (horizontal, by metric), VPA (right-sizing requests), Cluster Autoscaler (node scaling), and why HPA+VPA on the same resource/metric conflict; custom and external metrics; resource quotas for fairness; cost impact. Key claims to verify: 86% of cloud environments lack HPA, quietly inflating spend; combining the three is the recommended data-driven approach. Primary sources: KodeKloud Kubernetes best practices; ScaleOps/Spot cost-optimization 2026. End with a config matrix and the HPA/VPA conflict workaround.

topics.titleEn: Secrets Management in the Cloud, Done Right
topics.angle: how-to + moving off .env files to managed secrets with rotation and least privilege
topics.brief: Contrarian: your .env file is a breach waiting to happen. Sections: why env files and committed secrets fail; managed options (cloud secret managers, Vault, SOPS-encrypted git); short-lived dynamic credentials; automatic rotation; scoping and audit. Key claims to verify: dynamic short-lived credentials beat long-lived keys; SOPS lets you keep encrypted secrets in git safely. Primary sources: HashiCorp Vault docs; Mozilla SOPS. End with a migration path from .env to a secret manager and a rotation checklist. No real secrets in examples.

topics.angle: comparison + rollout strategies, rollback speed, and cost tradeoffs
topics.brief: Question-led: which rollout fits your risk tolerance? Sections: blue-green (two full environments, instant switch, instant rollback, double cost); canary (gradual traffic shift, metric gates, cheaper, slower); rolling as the baseline; database-change coupling and feature-flag pairing. Key claims to verify: blue-green gives fastest rollback but doubles infra; canary limits blast radius via progressive traffic. Primary sources: Google/GitLab deployment-strategy docs; CNCF progressive-delivery (Argo Rollouts/Flagger). End with a decision table by team maturity and workload.

topics.angle: how-to + pool modes, sizing, and the RLS/session footguns behind a pooler
topics.brief: Contrarian: pooling silently breaks your tenant isolation. Sections: why Postgres connections are expensive; PgBouncer multiplexing (e.g. 500 app to 20 DB connections); pool modes (session/transaction/statement) and what each disables; the RLS footgun (shared role makes current_user useless behind a pooler); fix with SET LOCAL in a transaction. Key claims to verify: transaction pooling breaks session features; SET LOCAL resets tenant context safely. Primary sources: Bytebase Postgres RLS footguns; PgBouncer docs. Note PgBouncer 1.25.2 CVE-2026-6664 patch. End with sizing guidance.

topics.titleEn: The Staff Engineer Path: Beyond Senior
topics.angle: guide + scope, influence, and the writing habit that gets you promoted
topics.brief: Story of a senior who plateaued until they started writing. Sections: what Staff means (technical leadership without reports, cross-team multiplier); archetypes (tech lead, architect, solver, right hand); how to demonstrate scope; sponsorship vs mentorship; the RFC/design-doc habit as highest-leverage signal. Key claims to verify: writing design docs and RFCs is the single highest-leverage skill for Staff promotion; Staff total comp ~$400K-$800K at top firms. Primary sources: jobsbyculture Staff Engineer 2026; StaffEng.com. End with a 90-day plan to build a promotion case.

topics.titleEn: How to Write an Engineering RFC
topics.angle: how-to + a reusable template, tradeoff framing, and a review flow that converges
topics.brief: Field notes on RFCs that shipped vs stalled. Sections: when an RFC is worth it; a template (context, goals/non-goals, options with tradeoffs, decision, risks, rollout); writing for skimmers; running async review and closing debate; recording the decision. Key claims to verify: clear written technical communication is the top Staff-track skill; documented tradeoffs speed alignment. Primary sources: jobsbyculture Staff Engineer 2026; Oxide/Rust RFC process. End with a fill-in template and anti-patterns (solution-first, no non-goals, unbounded scope).

topics.angle: guide + using impact metrics and comp bands as leverage
topics.brief: Sections: researching bands (levels data, transparency laws); why you let them name a range first; building an impact dossier (latency, error rate, cost per request, revenue); the AI-skills wage premium; competing offers and total-comp components. Key claims to verify: AI-skilled workers earn a 56% wage premium (PwC 2025, up from 25%); documented production metrics strengthen negotiation; ask for the range instead of stating a number. Primary sources: HackerX tech salary guide 2026; PwC Global AI Jobs Barometer. End with a metrics template and scripts.

topics.titleEn: Building an AI-Proof Developer Skill Set
topics.angle: contrarian + what stays valuable when AI writes most of the code
topics.brief: Contrarian: chasing the newest framework is a losing bet; judgment isn't. Sections: skills that compound (system design, debugging, reviewing AI output, writing, product sense); the risk of over- or under-using AI; proving impact with metrics. Key claims to verify: recruiters value architect-level thinking and clear communication over framework mastery; the productive middle path is thoughtful AI integration, not rejection or blind acceptance. Primary sources: Rootstack tech skills 2026; zenvanriel AI career pathways 2026. End with a self-audit and a learning plan weighted to durable skills.

topics.titleEn: How to Run Effective Code Reviews
topics.angle: guide + scope, tone, speed, and reviewing AI-generated pull requests
topics.brief: Contrarian: nitpicking style wastes everyone's time; review for risk. Sections: what review is for (correctness, design, maintainability, not formatting a linter handles); small PRs and fast turnaround; tone and blocking vs non-blocking comments; author responsibilities; reviewing AI-generated PRs skeptically. Key claims to verify: fast, focused reviews reduce cycle time; AI-written PRs need extra scrutiny as AI slop rises. Primary sources: Google engineering code-review guidelines; SmartBear/industry review data. End with a reviewer checklist and a comment-severity convention.

topics.titleEn: Building in Public: A Developer's Playbook
topics.angle: guide + audience, cadence, and what to share without oversharing
topics.brief: Question-led: does building in public actually help a dev career? Sections: why it compounds (network, hiring signal, feedback); what to share (progress, learnings, metrics) vs what to keep private (secrets, unshipped strategy); cadence and platform choice; turning a build log into a portfolio. Key claims to verify: visible work and clear communication are hiring signals in 2026; documenting impact aids promotion and negotiation. Primary sources: Rootstack tech skills 2026; indie-hacker build-in-public case studies. End with a lightweight weekly-sharing routine.
